description: Passing a value to a presence flag (e.g. --actionable-now true) fails with 'unknown flag true' because the parser reads the value as the next flag. The error blames the value rather than naming the misuse. Fix: when a BOOLEAN or NULL flag is immediately followed by a non-flag token throw a message that names the flag as a presence flag to be passed alone.
title: Presence flags reject a trailing value with a legible error
root_cause: parseFlags treats a presence flag's neighbouring token as the next flag; a stray value surfaces as a misleading unknown-flag error.
fix: In parseFlags after handling a BOOLEAN/NULL flag: if the next token exists and is not a known flag throw 'flag X is a presence flag; pass it with no value'.
acceptance_criteria: issue create with --actionable-now true exits non-zero with a message naming --actionable-now as a presence flag
--actionable-now passed alone still sets the field true
A known flag following --actionable-now is still parsed normally
